lesson.title: What Is a Program?
lesson.summary: Understand what a program is and run your first Go program.

sections.heading: Computers are obedient, not clever
sections.bodyHtml:
A computer does not understand your goals. It only does what you tell it, one instruction at a time.

A program is a precise recipe the machine follows. That is programming.

sections.heading: Where Go fits in
sections.bodyHtml:
You write Go source code in .go files. The Go compiler translates your text into instructions the machine runs.

You write main.go → go run → output on screen

sections.heading: Run your first program
sections.bodyHtml:
cd practice/hello
go run .

You should see Hello, world! — that is output.

sections.heading: Read the code
sections.bodyHtml:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    fmt.Println("Hello, world!")
}
  • package main — runnable program
  • import "fmt" — printing library
  • func main() — execution starts here

questions.prompt: A program is best described as:
Where does a Go program start executing?

exercises.title: Hello, world
exercises.instructions: Run go run . and confirm you see Hello, world!

exercises.title: Change the greeting
exercises.instructions: Run go run . and confirm you see Hello, Gopher!
